description: Populate the 9 ccb_navprove task directories with actual content: reference patches extracted from SWE-bench Pro config.json, Dockerfiles based on the same base images, and symptom-only instruction.md files with zero location leakage. First resolve qutebrowser and flipt task selection from the available SWE-bench Pro tasks.

userStories.title: Select 4 qutebrowser and 1 flipt source tasks from SWE-bench Pro
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want to identify which 4 qutebrowser tasks and which 1 flipt task from SWE-bench Pro to use as sources for navprove, since the originally-listed commit hashes don't match available tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Read tests/config.json for at least 20 qutebrowser tasks to examine their patch field (use python3 to parse JSON and count files changed in patch)
Select 4 qutebrowser tasks where: patch changes <= 5 files, the bug is functional (not UI/performance), and the symptom is describable without revealing file paths
Read tests/config.json for at least 10 flipt tasks to find one with a small patch and testable functional bug
Select 1 flipt task matching the same criteria

Create a mapping file at ralph-navprove-content/task_mapping.json with format: {"navprove-qb-url-001": "instance_qutebrowser-qutebrowser-XXXX", ...} for all 9 tasks (4 qutebrowser + ansible + teleport + vuls + flipt + tutanota)
The mapping file includes the confirmed source paths for ansible, teleport, vuls, and tutanota from progress.txt
python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('ralph-navprove-content/task_mapping.json')); assert len(m)==9" succeeds
userStories.notes: The 4 qutebrowser navprove tasks are named navprove-qb-url-001, navprove-qb-tab-001, navprove-qb-bookmark-001, navprove-qb-download-001 but the actual bugs don't need to match those names exactly — rename the navprove task dirs if the selected bugs are different. The key is finding 4 good qutebrowser bugs. For flipt, task is navprove-flipt-cache-001. Parse patch diffs with: python3 -c 'import json; c=json.load(open("tests/config.json")); patch=c.get("patch",""); files=set(l.split(" b/")[1] for l in patch.split("\n") if l.startswith("diff --git")); print(len(files), files)'

userStories.title: Populate 4 qutebrowser navprove tasks with content
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the 4 qutebrowser navprove tasks populated with reference patches, Dockerfiles, and symptom-only instruction.md files.
acceptanceCriteria: For each of the 4 qutebrowser navprove tasks (from task_mapping.json):
tests/reference_fix.patch contains the patch extracted from the source task's tests/config.json 'patch' field (valid git diff format, not empty)
environment/Dockerfile uses the same base image as the source task (FROM jefzda/sweap-images:...), adds pytest + pytest-timeout, sets WORKDIR appropriately
instruction.md follows the Find and Prove template, describes bug SYMPTOMS only, contains ZERO file paths or function names from the patch
grep -cE '\.(py|go|ts|js|rs)' instruction.md returns 0 for each task (no source file extensions as path references)
task.toml updated if task directory was renamed in US-001
Each tests/reference_fix.patch is valid: the first line starts with 'diff --git' or '---'
userStories.notes: To extract the patch: python3 -c 'import json; c=json.load(open("SOURCE/tests/config.json")); print(c["patch"])' > tests/reference_fix.patch. Read the source instruction.md to understand the bug, then rewrite as symptom-only. The SWE-bench Pro instruction.md often contains hints — you must strip ALL of them. Describe what a user would observe, not where in the code the bug is.

userStories.title: Populate ansible navprove task with content
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want navprove-ansible-vault-001 populated with reference patch, Dockerfile, and symptom-only instruction.md.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-ansible-vault-001/tests/reference_fix.patch contains the patch from the ansible source task's config.json (valid git diff, not empty)
benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-ansible-vault-001/environment/Dockerfile uses the ansible base image, adds pytest + pytest-timeout
benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-ansible-vault-001/instruction.md follows Find and Prove template with symptom-only description
grep -cE '\.(py|go|ts|js)' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-ansible-vault-001/instruction.md returns 0
tests/reference_fix.patch first line starts with 'diff --git' or '---'

userStories.title: Populate 3 Go navprove tasks with content (teleport, vuls, flipt)
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want the 3 Go navprove tasks populated with reference patches, Dockerfiles, and symptom-only instruction.md files.
acceptanceCriteria: For each of navprove-teleport-ssh-001, navprove-vuls-oval-001, navprove-flipt-cache-001:
tests/reference_fix.patch contains the patch from the source task's config.json (valid git diff, not empty)
environment/Dockerfile uses the source Go base image, ensures go test works
instruction.md follows Find and Prove template with symptom-only description, ZERO file paths or function names
grep -cE '\.(py|go|ts|js|rs)' instruction.md returns 0 for each task
Each tests/reference_fix.patch first line starts with 'diff --git' or '---'
task.toml updated if flipt task was renamed in US-001
userStories.notes: Source for teleport: benchmarks/ccb_swebenchpro/tasks/instance_gravitational-teleport-0415e422f12454db0c22316cf3eaa5088d6b6322. Source for vuls: instance_future-architect-vuls-139f3a81b66c47e6d8f70ce6c4afe7a9196a6ea8. Source for flipt: from task_mapping.json (selected in US-001).

userStories.title: Populate tutanota navprove task with content
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want navprove-tutanota-search-001 populated with reference patch, Dockerfile, and symptom-only instruction.md.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-tutanota-search-001/tests/reference_fix.patch contains the patch from the tutanota source task's config.json (valid git diff, not empty)
benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-tutanota-search-001/environment/Dockerfile uses the tutanota base image, ensures jest/vitest works
benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-tutanota-search-001/instruction.md follows Find and Prove template with symptom-only description
grep -cE '\.(py|go|ts|js)' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-tutanota-search-001/instruction.md returns 0
tests/reference_fix.patch first line starts with 'diff --git' or '---'

userStories.title: Verify all 9 tasks have complete, non-placeholder content
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want to verify that all 9 navprove tasks have real content (no TODO markers, no empty files, no placeholder text).
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'TODO' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-*/instruction.md returns 0 matches
grep -r 'TODO' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-*/environment/Dockerfile returns 0 matches
grep -r 'TODO' benchmarks/ccb_navprove/navprove-*/tests/reference_fix.patch returns 0 matches
All 9 instruction.md files are > 200 bytes (not placeholder stubs)
All 9 reference_fix.patch files are > 50 bytes (contain actual diffs)
All 9 Dockerfiles contain a FROM line

python3 -c "import pathlib; tasks=[p for p in pathlib.Path('benchmarks/ccb_navprove').iterdir() if p.is_dir() and p.name.startswith('navprove-')]; assert len(tasks)==9; [assert (t/'instruction.md').stat().st_size > 200 for t in tasks]" succeeds (syntax adjusted as needed)
userStories.notes: This is a QA gate. If any task still has placeholder content, fix it before marking this as passed.
